title: Unbounded ERC721Enumerable loops in claimTokens enable gas griefing and potential DoS
claim: The claimTokens() function and its helper getClaimableTokenAmountAndGammaToClaim() together perform six unbounded iterations over the caller's ERC721Enumerable balance across three NFT collections (alpha, beta, gamma). Each iteration makes external calls to balanceOf() (re-evaluated in the loop condition every cycle) and tokenOfOwnerByIndex(). The gas cost scales linearly with NFT count with no upper bound, allowing an attacker to grief victims by transferring many already-claimed NFTs to them, inflating their iteration count and causing excessive gas consumption or out-of-gas reverts.
impact: Users with large NFT holdings face economically burdensome gas costs to claim. An attacker can amplify this by dusting a victim with already-claimed NFTs (which still contribute to loop iterations without yielding any claimable tokens), potentially causing the victim's claim to revert and miss the time-limited claim window. After the window expires, unclaimed tokens are swept to the owner via claimUnclaimedTokens(). The FlawVerifier contract explicitly validates this exploit path.

paths.description: Dusting grief attack: attacker claims tokens, transfers already-claimed NFTs to victim, victim's claim consumes excessive gas
steps: 1. Attacker acquires many NFTs from alpha/beta/gamma collections (e.g., via NFTX vaults)
2. Attacker calls claimTokens() successfully using their own manageable NFT count
3. Attacker transfers all their (now already-claimed) NFTs to the victim address
4. Victim calls claimTokens() - the function iterates over all NFTs including dust
5. If victim has many NFTs, gas consumption becomes excessive (e.g., ~5M gas at N=500 per collection, ~10M gas at N=1000 per collection)
6. If gas exceeds what the victim provides, the transaction reverts
7. Victim may miss the claim window; unclaimed tokens go to the owner
paths.gasEstimate: ~5.1M gas at N=500 per collection; ~10.1M gas at N=1000 per collection. Block gas limit exhaustion requires N≈4000+ per collection (unrealistic), but economic griefing is viable at lower counts.

paths.description: FlawVerifier validated path: attacker claims seed NFTs, dusts victim, victim claim reverts under restricted gas cap
steps: 1. FlawVerifier.executeOnOpportunity() flash-borrows WETH, buys NFTX vault tokens, redeems NFTs
2. Attacker (FlawVerifier) claims their own tokens via claimTokens()
3. Attacker sweeps all owned NFTs to a ReceiverHelper victim contract
4. Victim calls claimTokens() with gas capped at gasleft() - 300,000
5. If the attacker's operations consumed most block gas, victim's restricted gas budget causes revert
6. FlawVerifier records outcome as 'validated:attacker_claims_seed_then_dusts_victim_then_victim_claim_reverts_under_block_gas_cap'

title: Gamma (BAKC) NFTs acquired after initial claim become permanently unclaimable due to alpha/beta pairing exhaustion
claim: The gamma claim amount is computed as gammaToBeClaim = min(unclaimedAlphaBalance + unclaimedBetaBalance, unclaimedGamaBalance). Once a user's alpha and beta NFTs are marked as claimed (alphaClaimed[tokenId] = true), any gamma NFTs acquired later can never be claimed because unclaimedAlphaBalance + unclaimedBetaBalance = 0. The require check at line 104 only verifies that the caller owns at least one alpha or beta NFT (not that they are unclaimed), so users can re-enter claimTokens() but will always receive 0 additional tokens for newly acquired gamma NFTs.
impact: Users who acquire additional BAKC (gamma) NFTs after their initial claim permanently lose the ability to claim airdrop tokens for those NFTs. This is an irreversible loss of entitled airdrop rewards tied to the gamma collection.

paths.description: Late gamma acquisition results in zero claimable tokens
steps: 1. User owns 5 alpha (BAYC) and 5 beta (MAYC) and 5 gamma (BAKC)
2. User calls claimTokens(): unclaimedAlphaBalance=5, unclaimedBetaBalance=5, unclaimedGamaBalance=5
3. gammaToBeClaim = min(10, 5) = 5, all gamma claimed successfully
4. User later acquires 3 more gamma NFTs (purchase, transfer, etc.)
5. User calls claimTokens() again: alphaClaimed/all are true, so unclaimedAlphaBalance=0, unclaimedBetaBalance=0
6. gammaToBeClaim = min(0, 3) = 0, user receives 0 additional tokens
7. The 3 newly acquired gamma NFTs can never be claimed for tokens

paths.description: Gamma-only holders cannot claim at all
steps: 1. User owns only gamma NFTs (no alpha or beta)
2. require((beta.balanceOf(msg.sender) > 0 || alpha.balanceOf(msg.sender) > 0)) fails
3. User can never enter claimTokens() despite owning gamma NFTs
4. This is by design (gamma requires alpha/beta pairing) but undocumented

title: claimTokens transfers zero tokens when all NFTs are already claimed, wasting gas and potentially reverting on non-standard ERC20 tokens
claim: When a user calls claimTokens() but all their NFTs are already claimed, tokensToClaim = 0. The function still executes all six loops (consuming gas proportional to NFT count) and then calls grapesToken.safeTransfer(msg.sender, 0). While most ERC20 implementations allow zero-value transfers, some tokens (notably certain USDC/USDT variants and custom tokens) revert on zero-value transfers, which would cause the entire transaction to revert unnecessarily.
impact: Users with only already-claimed NFTs waste gas on iterative loops that produce zero claimable tokens. If the GRAPES token reverts on zero-value transfers, such users cannot even execute the function to confirm they have nothing to claim.

steps: 1. User owns 100 alpha NFTs, all already claimed
2. User calls claimTokens()
3. getClaimableTokenAmountAndGammaToClaim iterates 100+ times, returns tokensToClaim=0
4. Three claim loops iterate another 300+ times, finding all tokens already claimed
5. grapesToken.safeTransfer(msg.sender, 0) transfers zero tokens
6. User paid gas for ~600 storage reads and external calls with zero benefit
